description:
You are acting as a junior penetration-tester who has just received a set of service banners that were gathered during a previous network reconnaissance phase.  
Your job is to perform a *very quick* vulnerability triage and write a concise log that another analyst can immediately consume.

1. The input data has already been placed for you at  
   /home/user/vuln_scan/input/banners.txt

2. Inside that file you will find one banner per line.  
   Current contents (do **not** modify them):

   80/tcp open  http  Apache httpd 2.2.15 (CentOS)  
   22/tcp open  ssh   OpenSSH 6.0p1 Debian 4+deb7u2 (protocol 2.0)  
   443/tcp open  ssl/openssl  OpenSSL 1.0.1e  
   21/tcp open  ftp   vsftpd 2.3.4

3. For the purpose of this exercise, treat the following versions as VULNERABLE:
   • Apache httpd < 2.4.0  
   • OpenSSH < 7.0  
   • Any OpenSSL 1.0.1 series release up to and including 1.0.1e  
   • vsftpd 2.3.4 (back-doored release)

4. Create the directory /home/user/vuln_scan/report/ (if it does not already exist).

5. Generate the file /home/user/vuln_scan/report/vulnerability_report.log  
   EXACTLY in the following strict format so automated grading can check it.  
   • First line: the literal string  
     VULNERABILITY REPORT  
   • For every vulnerable banner **in the same order they appear in banners.txt**, add one line formatted precisely as:  
     <PORT>/tcp - <SERVICE NAME> <VERSION> - VULNERABLE  
     Example (don’t wrap lines):  
     80/tcp - Apache httpd 2.2.15 - VULNERABLE  
   • After listing all vulnerable findings, add one final line that reads:  
     SCAN COMPLETE

6. Keep the solution simple—3 to 4 well-chosen shell commands (think: mkdir, grep/awk/sed, echo/printf, redirection, etc.) are all that is needed.

7. Do not add any extra lines, banner data, decorations, or blank lines to vulnerability_report.log; the grader does a byte-for-byte comparison.

When you are finished, /home/user/vuln_scan/report/vulnerability_report.log must exist and be readable by the current unprivileged user.
truth:
Directory that already exists before the task starts:
  /home/user/vuln_scan/input/

File that already exists before the task starts:
/home/user/vuln_scan/input/banners.txt  
Exact byte content (ending with a single trailing newline):
80/tcp open  http  Apache httpd 2.2.15 (CentOS)
22/tcp open  ssh   OpenSSH 6.0p1 Debian 4+deb7u2 (protocol 2.0)
443/tcp open  ssl/openssl  OpenSSL 1.0.1e
21/tcp open  ftp   vsftpd 2.3.4

Expected file that MUST be created by the agent:
/home/user/vuln_scan/report/vulnerability_report.log  
Exact byte content (ending with a single trailing newline):
VULNERABILITY REPORT
80/tcp - Apache httpd 2.2.15 - VULNERABLE
22/tcp - OpenSSH 6.0p1 - VULNERABLE
443/tcp - OpenSSL 1.0.1e - VULNERABLE
21/tcp - vsftpd 2.3.4 - VULNERABLE
SCAN COMPLETE

Permissions:
The directory /home/user/vuln_scan/report/ must be accessible (rwx------ or more open) to the current user.
The file vulnerability_report.log must be readable (rw------- or more open) by the current user.

No other files or directories are required for verification.
